title: Feature-first Architecture
objectives: Organize code by feature, not by layer
Keep vertical slices testable and swappable

sections.markdown: Feature-first architecture organizes the codebase by **domain feature** (auth, cart, orders) with each feature owning its full vertical slice — presentation, domain, data — instead of one giant top-level 'screens' and 'services' folder.

sections.code:
lib/
  core/                 (theme, router, DI, shared widgets)
  features/
    auth/               
       presentation/    (screens, view models)
       domain/          (entities, use cases, repo interfaces)
       data/            (repo impls, sources, DTOs, mappers)
    cart/    (same structure)
    orders/  (same structure)

rows: Change one feature in one folder
Read one feature end to end

sections.markdown: Feature-first scales to large teams because a feature is a **replaceable unit**. You can delete or refactor a feature in one directory; layers still exist — inside each feature — so Clean Architecture and MVVM still apply.
Put truly shared code in `core/` and be strict about what qualifies. If two features need to share code, hoist it — but resist premature 'utilities' that leak feature concerns.

flashcards.back: Layer-first groups by concern (all screens, all repos); feature-first groups by feature, each owning its own layers.
Features are replaceable units — change/delete a folder without touching others.

flashcards.front: Do you drop Clean Architecture with feature-first?
flashcards.back: No — layers still exist inside each feature (presentation/domain/data).

quiz.explain: Each feature owns its vertical slice.

options: Change locality: touch one folder for a feature
quiz.explain: Changes cluster inside the feature folder.

quiz.q: Where does truly shared code live?
quiz.explain: core/ or shared/ hosts cross-feature utilities.

challenges.text: Migrate one screen from layer-first to feature-first.
Move shared widgets/theme into core/ and verify no feature imports another.
Enforce feature isolation with a lint / import boundary rule.
Extract a feature into its own package and depend on it from the app module.

interviewQuestions: How does feature-first help large teams?
How do you decide what belongs in core/?
How do you prevent cross-feature imports?
